pattern: Sweeping days in order and always attending the available event that expires soonest is the greedy choice that maximises the count, made efficient with a min-heap of end days.
hints: On any given day, if you must pick one event to attend, the safest is the one that will become unavailable first — postponing the longer-lived ones loses nothing.
Process days from earliest to latest. Keep the set of events that have already started and not yet ended; among them the one ending soonest is the one to attend today.

Sort events by start day. As the day counter advances, push every newly started event's end day into a min-heap, discard ended events from the top, then pop the soonest-ending one to attend and increment the count.

edge_inputs.description: chain of overlapping events, all attendable
duplicate one-day options on the same days
three events all confined to two days, one must be dropped
a long event should yield its days to short ones
later-starting short event must be taken before a long one expires

stress.description: many random events over a moderate day range, forcing heavy heap churn
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    events = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s = rng.randint(1, 2000)
        e = rng.randint(s, min(2000, s + 50))
        events.append([s, e])
    return [events]
stress.note: bounded day span concentrates conflicts so the greedy is exercised

complexity.time: O(n log n + D log n)
